title: Weight and Mass
intro_hook: A school bag has the same mass on Earth and Moon, but its weight changes. Why?
big_idea: Mass is the amount of matter. Weight is the gravitational force on that mass. Weight depends on gravitational acceleration.

cause_process_result.cause: An object has Mass (amount of matter).
cause_process_result.process: A planet's gravity pulls on that mass.
cause_process_result.result: This pull is the object's Weight.

indian_analogies: Mass is like your height—it stays the same everywhere. Weight is like the effort it takes to jump—it's harder on Earth and easier on the Moon!

misconceptions.probe: Is weight really measured in kg in Physics? Or is kg for mass? What is the unit of force?

practice_problems.q: If mass (m) is 5 kg, and gravity (g) is 10 m/s², what is the Weight (W) in Newtons?
practice_problems.hint: Multiply mass by gravity: W = m × g.
